englishDefinitions: To sell all of a product that is in stock.
(intransitive) To abandon or betray one's principles to seek profit or other personal advantage; to abandon or betray one's supporters through this change.
(transitive) To betray (a person), usually a close friend or family member, for personal gain.
To sell one's business (with a connotative emphasis on entirety and finality).
(figurative) To commit or abandon oneself entirely (to a cause, a team, etc.).
persianTranslation: برای فروش تمام محصولات موجود در انبار.
(غیر گذرا) رها کردن یا خیانت به اصول خود برای کسب سود یا منفعت شخصی دیگر. رها کردن یا خیانت به حامیان خود از طریق این تغییر.
(متعدی) خیانت به (فردی)، معمولاً به دوست نزدیک یا یکی از اعضای خانواده، برای منافع شخصی.
برای فروش کسب و کار خود (با تأکید معنایی بر کامل بودن و نهایی بودن).
(تصویری) متعهد شدن یا رها کردن خود به طور کامل (به یک علت، یک تیم و غیره).
examples: They sold out of concert posters yesterday and won't get another shipment until next week.
It used to be a pleasant little community, until the leaders sold out to the developers.
He used to be a voice for the underdog until he sold out.
I'll kill him if I see him again; he sold me out.
The owners long resisted buyout offers, but when they were ready to retire, they finally sold out to a competitor.
